data.title: Missouri Man Masterminds $1,200,000 Check Fraud Scheme That Recruited Collaborators Over Instagram
data.description:
A Missouri man is headed to prison after orchestrating a bank fraud scheme using checks stolen in the mail. Last week, a US district judge sentenced Malik Jones, 28, to 42 months in prison after he pleaded guilty in 2024 to one count of bank fraud and one count of aggravated identity theft.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/09/24/missouri-man-masterminds-1200000-check-fraud-scheme-that-recruited-collaborators-over-instagram/

data.title: Nasdaq-Listed Helius Medical Technologies Bags Over 760,190 SOL For Its New $500 Million Solana Treasury
data.description:
Pantera-backed Helius Medical Technologies has purchased its first Solana tokens, marking the start of a corporate treasury reserve built around the sixth-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization. The company now holds approximately 760,190 tokens, valued at around $167 million, at an average cost of $231 per SOL. Shares of Helius Medical soared over 200% on Sept. […]
source: https://zycrypto.com/nasdaq-listed-helius-medical-technologies-bags-over-760190-sol-for-its-new-500-million-solana-treasury/

data.title: CZ: FT report is false, YZi Labs has not sought external financing
data.description: CZ responded to the Financial Times report on social media, calling it false and fabricated information. He emphasized that YZi Labs has not sought external funding and has not solicited or held discussions with any investors since its rebranding. The project has never produced or presented a pitch deck, nor does it have a so-called "demo." Furthermore, YZi Labs is not a spinoff from Binance. He further clarified that he pleaded guilty only to failing to maintain adequate anti-money laundering (AML) compliance procedures, not to "money laundering" as the media has alleged. The two are completely different. He stated that traditional media outlets are deliberately confusing "regulatory failure" with "theft." The founder also pointed out that the FT attempted to elicit a positive response through a lunch interview, but the resulting report did not meet the promised content, raising questions about the FT's ethics.

data.title: Powell: The tariffs are expected to be a one-time transmission effect and will end by the end of next year
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell said the impact of tariffs on consumer inflation is "not a major factor" and expects the tariffs to be a one-time transmission effect that will end by the end of next year.

data.title: Trump’s trade war hurts U.S. auto image as China wins over Europe
data.description:
Donald Trump’s tariff crusade is smashing what’s left of the U. S. auto sector’s reputation in Europe. New numbers show buyers across the continent are turning away from American cars and leaning into Chinese brands. That’s data straight from Escalent’s Chinese Automotive Brands Impact report, which ran from May 21 to July 31 and surveyed 1,692 […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-trade-war-us-auto-china-wins-europe/

data.title: White House considering nominating Josh Sterling for CFTC chairman
data.description: According to market news: The White House is considering nominating Josh Sterling as Chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Josh Sterling, formerly Director of the CFTC's Market Participants Division, is competing against competitors including Brian Quintenz, Director of Crypto Policy at a 16z; CFTC Commissioners Summer Mersinger and Caroline Pham; Perianne Boring, Founder and CEO of Digital Chamber; and Jill Sommers, former CFTC Commissioner.
